paper_metadata.title: A Generalist Agent
paper_metadata.abstract: Inspired by progress in large-scale language modeling, we apply a similar approach towards building a single generalist agent beyond the realm of text outputs. The agent, which we refer to as Gato, works as a multi-modal, multi-task, multi-embodiment generalist policy. The same network with the same weights can play Atari, caption images, chat, stack blocks with a real robot arm and much more, deciding based on its context whether to output text, joint torques, button presses, or other tokens. In this report we describe the model and the data, and document the current capabilities of Gato.

extraction_data.innovation: Gato's primary innovation is a single generalist agent that can perform many different tasks across diverse domains (control, language, vision) using a unified transformer architecture, trained autoregressively on tokenized sequences of observations and actions.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: DMLab, ALE Atari, Sokoban, BabyAI, DM Control Suite, MassiveText, M3W, ALIGN, MS-COCO Captions, Conceptual Captions, LTIP, OKVQA, VQAV2, and other multi-domain datasets (see Table 1)
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Task-specific fine-tuning with limited demonstration data
extraction_data.extension: Gato uses a specialized embedding function that handles different modalities (text, images, actions) with different embedding operations and position encodings for each modality.
extraction_data.research_problem: Creating a generalist agent that can perform well across multiple different tasks and domains without task-specific architectures
extraction_data.application: Robotics stacking tasks, Atari games, language tasks, and other diverse sequential decision-making problems
